annotations.label: 7 as a natural number

anchor.snippet: is the natural number following 6 and preceding 8

annotations.note: The number 7 is the standard `Nat` numeric literal `(7 : ℕ)`; e.g. used in `Nat.prime_seven`.

annotations.label: 7 is the only prime preceding a cube

anchor.snippet: It is the only prime number preceding a cube

annotations.note: No grep hit for a uniqueness theorem about primes p with p+1 a cube in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 7 is a Mersenne prime and double Mersenne prime

anchor.snippet: the fourth prime number, is not only a Mersenne prime

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `mersenne` and Lucas–Lehmer infrastructure but has no explicit theorem identifying 7 as a Mersenne or double-Mersenne prime.

annotations.label: 7 is the lowest natural number not a sum of three squares

anchor.snippet: Seven is the lowest natural number that cannot be represented as the sum of the squares of three integers

annotations.note: Mathlib has `SumTwoSquares` and `SumFourSquares` files but no sum-of-three-squares theorem (Legendre's three-square theorem).

annotations.label: Heptagon

anchor.snippet: A seven-sided shape is a heptagon

annotations.note: No occurrences of `heptagon` (or a definition of a regular 7-gon) found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heptagon is first non-constructible regular polygon

anchor.snippet: makes the heptagon the first regular polygon that cannot be directly constructed with these simple tools

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Constructible` files concern constructible sets/topologies, not straightedge-and-compass constructibility of polygons.

annotations.label: Ramanujan–Nagell equation

anchor.snippet: is the only number D for which the equation

annotations.note: No grep hit for Ramanujan–Nagell; the existing `Ramanujan` references are unrelated (modular forms).

annotations.label: Quadratic form representing all odd numbers

anchor.snippet: is one of seven numbers in the positive definite quadratic integer matrix representative of all odd numbers

annotations.note: No formalization of the classification of universal quadratic forms representing all odd numbers found.

annotations.label: Seven frieze groups

anchor.snippet: There are 7 frieze groups in two dimensions

annotations.note: No `frieze` or wallpaper-group classification found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heptagon plane-vertex tiling

anchor.snippet: it is one of fourteen polygons that can fill a plane-vertex tiling

annotations.note: No tilings/plane-vertex theorems found for the heptagon in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Max intersecting diagonals bound

anchor.snippet: the maximum number of intersecting diagonals (other than through its center) is at most 7

annotations.note: No combinatorial result about diagonals of a heptagon found.

annotations.label: 7-uniform Krotenheerdt tilings

anchor.snippet: there are precisely seven 7-uniform Krotenheerdt tilings

annotations.note: No `Krotenheerdt` or k-uniform tiling classification found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fano plane

anchor.snippet: the smallest possible finite projective plane, has 7 points and 7 lines

annotations.note: Mathlib has general finite projective plane / incidence configuration infrastructure but no dedicated `FanoPlane` definition.

annotations.label: Octonion square roots of −1

anchor.snippet: the octonions contain seven distinct square roots of

annotations.note: Octonions are only mentioned in passing comments (e.g. `HSpaces`); no `Octonion` type or square-root-of-(-1) theorem is formalized.

annotations.label: Seven-dimensional cross product

anchor.snippet: seven-dimensional vectors have a cross product

annotations.note: `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.CrossProduct` defines `crossProduct` only on `Fin 3 → R`; no 7-dimensional analogue exists.

annotations.label: Lowest exotic sphere dimension

anchor.snippet: The lowest known dimension for an exotic sphere is the seventh dimension

annotations.note: Mathlib only declares Milnor's exotic 7-sphere as a `proof_wanted` statement, not a proven theorem.

annotations.label: Hypercompact Vinberg polytopes

anchor.snippet: is the highest dimension for non-simplex hypercompact Vinberg polytopes

annotations.note: No `Vinberg` or hyperbolic Coxeter polytope classification found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Seven elementary catastrophes

anchor.snippet: There are seven fundamental types of catastrophes

annotations.note: No `catastrophe` or Thom catastrophe-theory classification found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Probability of rolling 7 on two dice

anchor.snippet: seven has a 1 in 6 probability of being rolled, the greatest of any number

annotations.note: No dice-sum probability computation found in Mathlib's probability library.

annotations.label: Opposite faces of a die sum to 7

anchor.snippet: The opposite sides of a standard six-sided die always add to 7

annotations.note: This is a convention about physical dice, not a mathematical theorem; no relevant Mathlib content.

annotations.label: Millennium Prize Problems

anchor.snippet: The Millennium Prize Problems are seven problems in mathematics

annotations.note: No grep hit for `Millennium`; individual problems (e.g. `PoincareConjecture`) exist but the seven-problem collection is not a Mathlib concept.

annotations.label: Reciprocal of 7 repeats six digits

anchor.snippet: 999,999 divided by 7 is exactly 142,857

annotations.note: No theorem about the decimal-period length of 1/7 found in Mathlib.
